channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Title favourite Sabalenka lets another Slam opportunity slip from her grasp
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka would not say it, but there is an inescapable feeling the world number one has lost another golden Grand Slam opportunity after her shock French Open loss.

item.title: Cobolli sets up all-Italian French Open semi-final
item.description: Flavio Cobolli battles past Felix Auger-Aliassime to reach his first major semi-final and ensure an Italian will play in Sunday's French Open showpiece.

item.title: Sabalenka 'fell into dark hole mentally' in shock quarter-final loss
item.description: World number one Aryna Sabalenka sees her French Open title hopes vanish as she unravels in a crushing quarter-final defeat by Diana Shnaider.

item.title: Qualifier Chwalinska reaches semi-finals to continue dream French Open run
item.description: Qualifier Maja Chwalinska continued her dream French Open run as she beats Anna Kalinskaya to reach her first Grand Slam semi-final.

item.title: Hewett & Reid into wheelchair doubles semi-finals
item.description: British duo Alfie Hewett and Gordon Reid start their bid for a seventh consecutive men's wheelchair doubles title at the French Open with a comfortable quarter-final win.

item.title: GB's Patten into Paris semi-finals with Heliovaara
item.description: Henry Patten and Harri Heliovaara reach the French Open men's doubles semi-finals as they continue their bid for a third Grand Slam title together.

item.title: 'I want to give this match to Ukraine' - Kostyuk beats Svitolina to reach last four
item.description: Marta Kostyuk dedicates her match against compatriot Elina Svitolina to Ukraine after becoming the first woman from her nation to reach the singles semi-finals at Roland Garros.

item.title: Zverev ends Jodar run to reach semi-finals and keep Grand Slam dream alive
item.description: Alexander Zverev keeps his pursuit of an elusive Grand Slam title on track with a comprehensive quarter-final win against teenager Rafael Jodar at the French Open.

item.title: Britain's Hewett into quarter-finals but Reid loses
item.description: Alfie Hewett is through to the quarter-finals of the men's wheelchair singles but fellow Briton Gordon Reid loses his first-round match at the French Open.

item.title: French Open - schedule, seedings and how to follow
item.description: The second Grand Slam of the year is under way in Paris - here's all you need to know about the French Open.

item.title: Reborn Berrettini one of three Italians into last eight
item.description: A beaming Matteo Berrettini says tennis is the "love of my life" after reaching a Grand Slam quarter-final for the first time in almost four years.

item.title: Will Sabalenka and Osaka open door for women's night sessions?
item.description: It may only have lasted 87 minutes, but Aryna Sabalenka's match against Naomi Osaka brought an X-factor to a very rare French Open women's night-session match.

item.title: Williams, 44, to return to action in Queen's doubles
item.description: Serena Williams will return to competitive tennis for the first time in nearly four years when she plays in the women's doubles at Queen's.

item.title: No previous French Open champions left - who will seize chance?
item.description: With Iga Swiatek's early French Open exit meaning new men's and women's champions will be crowned, BBC Sport analyses how the draws have been blown open.

item.title: Sabalenka's superior serve helps her past spirited Osaka
item.description: World number one Aryna Sabalenka advances to her fourth consecutive French Open quarter-final with a hard-fought night session win over Naomi Osaka.

item.title: Vallejo fined for 'unacceptable' comments about female umpire
item.description: Adolfo Daniel Vallejo is fined $65,000 (£48,300) for making "unacceptable" comments about a female umpire following his French Open exit.

item.title: Fonseca, 19, shows title credentials by backing up Djokovic win
item.description: Rising star Joao Fonseca showcases his title credentials with victory over two-time finalist Casper Ruud to book his place in the French Open quarter-finals.

item.title: Kostyuk beats four-time French Open winner Swiatek
item.description: Four-time French Open champion Iga Swiatek is knocked out in the fourth round as Marta Kostyuk continues her superb run of form.

item.title: Sabalenka v Osaka first French Open women's night match since 2023
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka's fourth-round match against Naomi Osaka on Monday will be the first women's match to take place in the French Open's night-time slot since 2023.

item.title: Why have women been overlooked for French Open night matches?
item.description: The first eight of the French Open's night sessions this year have been allocated to men's matches.

item.title: Defending champion Gauff latest big name knocked out of French Open
item.description: Defending champion Coco Gauff is out of the French Open after a third-round defeat by Anastasia Potapova.

item.title: Ukrainian makes impassioned speech after facing Russian
item.description: Ukraine's Oleksandra Oliynykova says she cannot "stay silent" as she continues to heavily criticise Russian players for not denouncing the war.

item.title: The clearest sign Father Time is closing in on Djokovic?
item.description: Why Novak Djokovic's French Open exit is another golden opportunity lost for the 39-year-old as a new Grand Slam era dawns.

item.title: Advertising blocks moved after players air concerns
item.description: The position of on-court advertising blocks were moved at the French Open on Saturday, but not removed - as some players had demanded for safety reasons.

item.title: Sabalenka suffers shock French Open loss to Shnaider
item.description: Watch highlights as world number one Aryna Sabalenka crumbled against Diana Shnaider, losing 3-6 7-5 6-0 in another French Open shock result.

item.title: 'My fault to hire him' - Davidovich Fokina 'abandoned' by coach in French Open
item.description: Spain's Alejandro Davidovich Fokina was left without a coach midway through the French Open after Mariano Puerta abruptly quit by text message. The split came hours after Davidovich Fokina's five-set first round victory over Damir Dzumhur.

item.title: 'I hope to inspire' - Monfils bows out of final French Open
item.description: Gael Monfils speaks of his desire to inspire young black kids to play tennis, after exiting his final French Open, his home Grand Slam and the major where he reached the semi-finals in 2008 and a further three quarter-finals.

item.title: Sinner completes 'Golden Masters' with win in Rome
item.description: Watch highlights as world number one Jannik Sinner beats Norway's Casper Ruud 6-4 6-4 to win the Italian Open and complete the full set of nine ATP Masters 1000 titles - known as the 'career Golden Masters'.

item.title: Seven-time Grand Slam champion Murray retires
item.description: Seven-time Grand Slam champion Jamie Murray retires from tennis as one of the most-decorated doubles players in the world. Here's a look back at his 2007 and 2017 mixed doubles triumphs at Wimbledon.

item.title: Why beating Djokovic is coming of age moment for Fonseca
item.description: Why Joao Fonseca's five-set French Open victory over the legendary Novak Djokovic shows the 19-year-old has arrived at the top of tennis.

item.title: Travelling solo and sharing rooms - what is life like on tennis’ lower rung?
item.description: Sleeping in a transit van is one of the money-saving tricks used by those on the lower rung of the tennis circuit.

item.title: 'In the end, I gave my all' - Wawrinka bids fond farewell to French Open
item.description: Stan Wawrinka bids a fond farewell to the French Open as the 2015 champion is beaten in the first round in Paris.

item.title: 'A big ask' in Paris - will a stronger Raducanu emerge at Wimbledon?
item.description: Emma Raducanu's French Open first-round defeat was not unexpected, but the nature of it may have left her wishing she had stayed at home.

item.title: 'I broke the curse' - the fall and rise of a teenage Grand Slam champion
item.description: When 19-year-old Bianca Andreescu stunned Serena Williams to win the US Open it was unthinkable it would be more than 2,300 days before her next triumph.

item.title: Five sets for women? More entertainment? - where does 'Glastonbury of tennis' go next?
item.description: Once dubbed the 'Happy Slam' by Roger Federer, the innovative Australian Open has gone from strength to strength - but there have been signs of discontent.

item.title: Happy tennis, serious name - the making of Carlos Alcaraz
item.description: Before Carlos Alcaraz became the youngest man to complete the career Grand Slam, he was simply Carlitos from Murcia. This is his story.

item.title: Murray retires after 'tough year of tennis'
item.description: Seven-time Grand Slam champion Jamie Murray retires from tennis, saying he had no desire to return to the sport and has no regrets.

item.title: That's for you Rory! - Alcaraz toasts win with golf celebration
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz beats Joao Fonseca in the Men's singles at the Miami Open, and celebrates with a golf swing in front on the spectating Rory McIllroy.

item.title: Draper cruises to Davis Cup win on return from injury
item.description: Jack Draper cruises to an emphatic victory on his long-awaited return from injury to give Great Britain the lead over Norway in Davis Cup qualifying.

item.title: 'I'm always the chaser?' - Djokovic unimpressed by reporter's question
item.description: 24-time Grand Slam winner Novak Djokovic says he felt "disrespectful" after being asked how he felt "chasing" Jannik Sinner and Carlos Alcaraz, after previously "chasing" Roger Federer and Rafael Nadal during the early parts of his career.

item.title: 'Are we animals in the zoo?' - Top tennis players call for more privacy
item.description: Jessica Pegula, Iga Swiatek and Novak Djokovic have all supported Coco Gauff's concerns over the use of cameras away from the court at the Australian Open, insisting more privacy is needed for all players competing.

item.title: 'I don't want to give myself too much of a hard time'
item.description: British number one Emma Raducanu says she is leaving the Australian Open with her "head held high" after losing 7-6 (7-3) 6-2 to Austria's Anastasia Potapova in the second round.

item.title: Watch: Amateur beats Sinner in One Point Slam title win
item.description: Watch amateur tennis player beat men's world number two Jannik Sinner and women's world number 117 Joanna Garland to earn A$1m (£496,835) in Australian Open's new Million Dollar One Point Slam.

item.title: Kyrgios defeats Sabalenka in 'Battle of the Sexes'
item.description: Watch the best moments between Aryna Sabalenka and Nick Kyrgios at the Battle of the Sexes in Dubai.

item.title: 'Point of the match!' - Sabalenka hits 'amazing' winner against Kyrgios
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka hits an 'amazing' return against Nick Kyrgios at the Battle of the Sexes in Dubai.

item.title: 1973 Battle of the Sexes was about social change - King
item.description: American tennis legend Billie Jean King says the upcoming 'Battle of the Sexes' match between Aryna Sabalenka and Nick Kyrgios is "not the same" as her 1973 victory over Bobby Riggs.

item.title: Sabalenka confident 'Battle of the Sexes' won't damage women's sport
item.description: BBC Sport speaks to Aryna Sabalenka and Nick Kyrgios about their controversial Battle of the Sexes-style match, set to take place in Dubai on 28 December.

item.title: Women's tennis going in right direction - King
item.description: Billie Jean King, founder of the WTA, believes the organisation's billion-dollar deal with Mercedes shows the tour "continues to lead the way" in women's sport.

item.title: Who has won tennis' 'Battle of the Sexes'?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team details the history of 'Battle of the Sexes' matches in tennis

item.title: What is US Open prize money?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at how much prize money is on offer at the US Open.

item.title: Why is there a pineapple on Wimbledon's men's trophy?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at why there is a pineapple on the top of the Wimbledon trophy for the winner of the men's singles.

item.title: Why were there no line judges at Wimbledon 2025?
item.description: Wimbledon are relying on electronic line calling to judge whether a ball is in or out.
